<div style="--fade-in-animation: fade-in-new-response 150ms cubic-bezier(0.3,0,0.8,0.15); animation: auto ease 0s 1 normal none running none; padding-top: 30px; position: relative; opacity: 1;"><div><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; z-index: 1; margin-top: 0px;"><div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>the history of blockchain tech</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"></div><div></div></div></div><div id="aim-chrome-initial-inline-async-container"><div><div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">The history of blockchain technology began long before Bitcoin, with foundational cryptographic concepts developed in the 1980s and 1990s</div>. While Satoshi Nakamoto popularized the technology in 2008, it was built on decades of research into decentralized systems and secure timestamping.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 79.2891px;"><img id="img-P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Wikipedia" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Wikipedia</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+3</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">1. Foundational Concepts (1982–2004)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Before a functional blockchain existed, researchers developed the mathematical "building blocks" required for decentralized trust.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">1982: Early Protocols:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Cryptographer<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_2" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=david+chaum&amp;kgmid=%2Fm%2F01jq87&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQIBRAC&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">David Chaum</a></span></div></span></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>proposed a blockchain-like protocol in his dissertation, focusing on maintaining trust among "mutually suspicious groups".</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">1991–1992: Cryptographic Timestamps:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Researchers<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_3" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=stuart+haber&amp;kgmid=%2Fg%2F11fywbzdt1&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQIBRAF&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">Stuart Haber</a></span></div></span></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_4" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=w.+scott+stornetta&amp;kgmid=%2Fg%2F11l7hdggsj&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQIBRAH&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">W. Scott Stornetta</a></span></div></span></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>introduced a system for timestamping digital documents so they could not be tampered with or backdated. In 1992, they incorporated<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Merkle trees</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>into the design, allowing multiple documents to be "collected into one block" for better efficiency.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">1997: Proof of Work:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_5" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=adam+back&amp;kgmid=%2Fm%2F02q93bn&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQIBRAK&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">Adam Back</a></span></div></span></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>released<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Hashcash</strong>, a proof-of-work algorithm originally designed to limit email spam. This mechanism later became the heart of Bitcoin’s mining process.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">1998–2004: Pre-Bitcoin Prototypes:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Computer scientists like<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_6" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=nick+szabo&amp;kgmid=%2Fm%2F0zm_9kb&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQIBRAN&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">Nick Szabo</a></span></div></span></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(Bit Gold) and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_7" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=hal+finney&amp;kgmid=%2Fm%2F080gwtb&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQIBRAP&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">Hal Finney</a></span></div></span></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(Reusable Proof of Work) developed early digital cash systems that moved closer to the modern blockchain model.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">2. The Birth of Bitcoin (2008–2009)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">Satoshi Nakamoto</a></span></div></span></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>combined these earlier technologies to solve the "double-spending problem" without needing a central bank.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">October 2008:</strong><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><span>&nbsp;</span><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_26" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=nakamoto&amp;kgmid=%2Fm%2F011bpfmq&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQICRAC&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">Nakamoto</a></span></div><span>&nbsp;</span>published the<span>&nbsp;</span></span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://bitcoin.org/bitcoin.pdf" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">Bitcoin Whitepaper</a></span><span>&nbsp;</span>titled<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">"Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System"</em>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">January 2009:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The Bitcoin network went live with the mining of the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Genesis Block</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(Block #0), which contained a message referencing a bank bailout during the financial crisis.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">January 2009:</strong><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><span>&nbsp;</span>The first-ever bitcoin transaction occurred when Nakamoto sent 10 BTC to<span>&nbsp;</span><div style="display: inline;"><span><a id="P-C-acGRHaS6kPIPnuLAqA4_27" tabindex="0" role="button" href="https://www.google.com/search?q=hal+finney&amp;kgmid=%2Fm%2F080gwtb&amp;sa=X&amp;ved=2ahUKEwiBrYLKzLGTAxUkHUQIHR4xEOUQ3egRegQICRAH&amp;gl=us" style="color: unset; text-decoration: underline 10% dotted rgb(139, 140, 145); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 12%; outline: 0px;">Hal Finney</a></span></div></span>.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; animation: var(--fade-in-animation); padding-top: 30px; position: relative; margin-top: 24px;"><div><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; z-index: 1; margin-top: 0px;"><div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>what are smart contracts, how do they work technically</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"></div><div></div></div></div><div><div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Smart contracts are self-executing digital agreements where the terms are written directly into lines of code</div>. They run on a blockchain, meaning they execute automatically when predefined conditions are met, without the need for a central authority or "middleman" like a bank or lawyer.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 140.43px;"><img id="img-RPe-aa2QI8jQkPIPv-SdsA8_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Internet Policy Review" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Internet Policy Review</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+3</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">The classic technical analogy is a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">vending machine</strong>: if you insert the correct amount of money (the condition), the machine automatically dispenses the snack (the action).<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 108.039px;"><img id="img-RPe-aa2QI8jQkPIPv-SdsA8_2" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Bloomberg Law" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Bloomberg Law</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">How They Work Technically<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">The technical lifecycle of a smart contract moves through four key stages:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ol style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 20px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Development &amp; Compilation</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">High-Level Code:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Developers write the contract in specialized languages such as<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Solidity</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(for Ethereum) or<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Rust</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(for Solana).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Bytecode:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Because blockchain virtual machines cannot read high-level code directly, the script is compiled into low-level<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">bytecode</strong>.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Deployment</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">On-Chain Storage:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>A user sends a special transaction to the blockchain containing this bytecode. Once the transaction is confirmed, the code is stored at a unique address on the ledger.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Immutability:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Once deployed, the code is "immutable"—it generally cannot be changed or deleted, ensuring the rules of the agreement are permanent.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Execution via Virtual Machine</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Triggers:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The contract sits "dormant" until a user or another contract sends a transaction to its address that triggers a specific function.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Virtual Machine (EVM):</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>On Ethereum, the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>acts as a distributed "supercomputer" that processes the bytecode using specific<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">opcodes</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(instructions like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">ADD</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">STORE</code>, or<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">CALL</code>).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Gas Fees:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>To prevent infinite loops or spam, every computational step requires "Gas," a small fee paid in the network's native cryptocurrency.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Consensus &amp; State Change</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Network Validation:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Every<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">node</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(computer) in the network runs the code independently to verify the result.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Finality:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>If the nodes reach a consensus that the execution followed the rules, the "state" of the blockchain (e.g., account balances or ownership records) is updated permanently across the entire network.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 124.547px;"><img id="img-RPe-aa2QI8jQkPIPv-SdsA8_18" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Gemini Exchange" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Gemini Exchange</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+12</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul></li></ol><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Key Technical Characteristics<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Deterministic:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Given the same input, a smart contract will always produce the exact same output on every node.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Isolated:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Execution usually happens in a "sandbox" (like the EVM), meaning the code cannot access files or processes on the host computer for security reasons.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Oracles:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Since smart contracts cannot natively "see" the outside world (like the weather or stock prices), they use third-party services called<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Oracles</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>to feed external data into the blockchain.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 108.039px;"><img id="img-RPe-aa2QI8jQkPIPv-SdsA8_19" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Bloomberg Law" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Bloomberg Law</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+4</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Would you like to see a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">real-world code example</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>of a simple contract, or should we discuss the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">security risks</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>involved in writing them?<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; Because storing large amounts of data (like a 10,000-word blog post or a video) directly on a blockchain is extremely expensive, these apps use a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">hybrid model</strong>:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Decentralized Storage:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Tools like<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">IPFS</span><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Arweave</span><span>&nbsp;</span>store the actual files, code, or images.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; padding: 4px 20px 16px;"><div role="button" tabindex="0" id="rw0ISc" style="align-items: stretch; border-radius: 9999px; color: var(--jINu6c); cursor: pointer; display: flex; justify-content: center; overflow: hidden; position: relative; background-color: var(--aYn2S); outline: 0px;"><span style="font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; font-weight: 400; margin: 8px 16px;">Show all</span></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div></div></div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div></div></div></div><div style="--fade-in-animation: fade-in-new-response 150ms cubic-bezier(0.3,0,0.8,0.15); animation: var(--fade-in-animation); padding-top: 30px; position: relative; margin-top: 24px;"><div><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; z-index: 1; margin-top: 0px;"><div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>without massive fund for the free tier, the storage would be very unreliable i think.</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"></div><div></div></div></div><div><div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">You’re hitting on the biggest hurdle for decentralized apps (dApps): the<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">"Storage Gap."</strong><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">You're right—if you tried to store a video or a large codebase directly on a smart contract, the "Gas" fees would cost thousands of dollars. To solve this without a massive central fund, the industry uses<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Decentralized Storage Networks</strong>, which handle the "unreliability" problem differently than a standard free tier.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Here is how they try to stay reliable without a central "Big Tech" server:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">1. The "Hard Drive Rental" Model (Filecoin/Sia)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Instead of a company like Google giving you space, these protocols create a global marketplace.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it stays reliable:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>You pay a tiny amount of crypto to "rent" space from hundreds of individual providers globally.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The "Proof":</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The smart contract requires providers to constantly submit<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">"Proof of Spacetime."</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>If their server goes offline or they lose your data, the smart contract automatically takes their "collateral" (money they deposited) and pays it to you as a penalty.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">2. The "Permanent Web" Model (Arweave)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">This is the closest thing to a "reliable free tier" logic, but it uses a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">one-time endowment</strong>.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Math:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>You pay a one-time fee upfront (usually a few cents for a document).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Most of that fee goes into a "Storage Endowment." The interest earned on that pool pays miners to keep your data alive for 200+ years. As hard drive tech gets cheaper over time, the math suggests the data stays online forever.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">3. Content Addressing (IPFS)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">In a "Twitter" or "WordPress" app built on IPFS, your data isn't at a URL like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">://twitter.com</code>. It has a unique fingerprint (a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">CID</strong>).<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Reliability Issue:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>If nobody "pins" (hosts) your data, it disappears.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Solution:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Communities or DAO (Decentralized Autonomous Organization) treasuries often "pin" important data collectively so no single person carries the cost.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">The Reality Check<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">You're correct that "free" is hard. Most Web3 apps today are<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">subsidized</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>by VC funding or developer grants to make the user experience feel like "Free Twitter." If that funding runs out and the users aren't willing to pay a fraction of a cent per post, the data could indeed become "unreliable" or disappear.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Would you like to see how much it actually<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">costs</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>to store a standard 200-page book on these networks compared to AWS?<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div><div style="clear: both; display: flex; flex-flow: wrap; row-gap: 16px; margin-block: 18px 0px; justify-content: space-between;"><div style="display: contents;"></div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; flex-shrink: 1; width: 652px; align-items: center;"><div style="display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 4px;"><div style="display: flex; inline-size: 44px;"><div style="position: relative; display: inline-flex;"><button style="margin: 0px; background-color: var(--xhUGwc); display: flex; justify-content: center;"><span style="inline-size: 20px; block-size: 20px;"><div></div></span></button></div></div></div><div><div><div id="shrproxy38" style="display: flex; inline-size: 44px;"><button style="margin: 0px; animation: var(--fade-in-animation); padding-top: 30px; position: relative; margin-top: 24px; min-height: var(--aim-container-min-height);"><div><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>most involve deeply on crypto currency, not ideal for general purpose apps</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"></div><div></div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px; color: rgb(230, 232, 240); font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-style: normal; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: normal; orphans: 2; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; widows: 2; word-spacing: 0px; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; white-space: normal; background-color: rgb(16, 18, 24); text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">You’ve hit the nail on the head. This is the<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">"Crypto Friction"</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>problem. For a regular user, having to buy ETH, set up a MetaMask wallet, and pay a "gas fee" just to post a tweet or edit a document is a total dealbreaker. It kills the "free tier" experience people expect.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">To bridge this gap for apps like GitHub or WordPress, developers are moving toward<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">"Invisible Blockchain"</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>architectures that try to hide the crypto entirely:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">1. Account Abstraction (ERC-4337)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">This is the tech that lets users sign up with an<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">email and password</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(or FaceID) instead of a 24-word seed phrase.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>A "Paymaster" (the app developer) covers the transaction fees in the background.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Result:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The user experiences a "Free Tier," and the developer pays the cents-per-action costs behind the scenes, just like they’d pay a server bill to AWS.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">2. Layer 2 &amp; Sidechains<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Networks like<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Base</strong>,<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Arbitrum</strong>, or<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Polygon</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>make transactions so cheap (often less than $0.01) that an app can easily subsidize thousands of user actions without a "massive fund."<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">3. Off-Chain "Lazy" Minting<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Instead of putting every "Like" or "Edit" on the blockchain instantly:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">The app records actions on a fast, free off-chain database.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">It only "settles" the data to the blockchain in batches or when a user wants to "claim" their data.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">This keeps the app feeling fast and free, while still giving you the "ownership" benefits of blockchain.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">4. Decentralized Identity (DID)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Imagine using one "Universal Login" that works for GitHub, Twitter, and WordPress. You don't need a currency for this—just a cryptographic key. The blockchain only stores the "proof" that you are you, while the actual app remains free to use.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Reality:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>You're right—if a project requires you to be a "crypto trader" just to use a word processor, it will never reach general adoption. The most successful "Web3" apps are the ones where you<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">don't even realize</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>a blockchain is running in the basement.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Do you think a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">universal login</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(one key for all sites) is enough of a benefit to make people switch from Google/Facebook logins, or is the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">free-and-easy</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>status quo too strong?<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div><div style="clear: both; display: flex; flex-flow: wrap; row-gap: 16px; margin-block: 18px 0px; justify-content: space-between;"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="margin-block: 18px 0px; display: inline-block; align-self: center; font-size: 14px; color: var(--IXoxUe); flex-grow: 1; font-weight: 500; line-height: 20px; margin: 0px 8px 0px 0px;">AI responses may include mistakes. For financial advice, consult a professional.<span>&nbsp;</span><a href="https://support.google.com/websearch?p=aimode&amp;gl=us" style="color: var(--IXoxUe); text-decoration: underline dotted; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); white-space: nowrap; line-height: 20px; outline: 0px;">Learn more</a></div></div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; flex-shrink: 1; width: 652px; align-items: center;"><div style="display: flex; align-items: center;
